briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for July 6, 2026. The climate-related news collected today once again highlights the severity of the global climate crisis, while also showcasing diverse domestic and international efforts to combat it.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Threatens Daily Life and Lives
sections.content: Recently, record-breaking heatwaves, large-scale wildfires, and storms have frequently occurred worldwide, causing casualties and economic losses [2, 3, 14, 18, 26, 34, 35, 157, 160, 169, 172]. In particular, at least 25 people have died in the United States due to extreme heat [35], and thousands have been forced to evacuate due to wildfires spreading across Southern Europe [172]. The potential recurrence of a super El Niño, which caused 50 million deaths approximately 150 years ago, also reminds us of the long-term threat of the climate crisis [24]. Domestically, warning signs are growing across the ecosystem, including damage to farmlands from algal blooms [1], the appearance of blue crabs [10], and recurring concerns about bee disappearances [17]. Research has also been published indicating that more species are going extinct in temperate regions [159, 167].

sections.title: ⚠️ Reality of Domestic Climate Action: The Gap Between Goals and Execution
sections.content: Despite efforts by the government and corporations to achieve carbon neutrality goals, several challenges are emerging in the actual implementation process. Only 13 local governments nationwide have dedicated carbon neutrality organizations, and 38.5% of local governments received a 'D' grade in their carbon neutrality plan evaluations, indicating that the foundation for climate administration remains weak [39, 50, 52, 79]. Furthermore, despite the expansion of renewable energy supply, greenhouse gas emissions have not decreased, putting the achievement of carbon neutrality in the advanced industrial era to the test [60, 108]. Some companies, despite publishing sustainability reports, have seen increased greenhouse gas emissions and '0' renewable energy usage for three consecutive years, clearly showing a gap between declarations and actual actions [117].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts for Climate Adaptation and Sustainable Society Building
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are underway for climate change adaptation. Kakao and the Korea Meteorological Administration have signed an MOU for responding to hazardous weather [4], Gimje City has initiated research for a master plan to strengthen agricultural competitiveness [5, 13, 23, 27], and Gyeongsangnam-do has secured the largest national budget for climate change response aquaculture projects [9, 11], thereby enhancing the resilience of vulnerable sectors. The 'Climate Change Safety Park,' a 2.1 billion won project, has been completed [7], and Eulji University is training military ecological and environmental experts [21], strengthening overall societal adaptive capacity. Various companies, including CJ Logistics, are publishing sustainability reports and advancing their ESG and climate disclosure response systems [15, 19, 22, 25, 28, 30, 31, 32, 33, 36], while institutions like iM Financial Group are actively promoting carbon neutrality campaigns with citizens, encouraging changes in daily life [42, 43, 44, 48, 53, 54, 59, 73, 84, 97]. Internationally, direct adaptation efforts to ecosystem changes are also observed, such as Greece installing floating barriers to prevent the influx of toxic fish species [173].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Adopting Innovative Technologies
sections.content: Domestic and international efforts to transition to renewable energy are steadily accelerating. Hanwha Q CELLS has secured a project to build a 200MW solar power plant in Gibson County, Indiana, USA, which will supply renewable energy to global big tech company Meta [107, 109, 114, 116, 120, 121, 123, 127, 130, 131, 132, 133, 135, 136, 137, 138, 139, 140, 141, 143, 145, 146, 147, 148, 149, 151, 152, 154, 155, 156], and Korea Southern Power is actively pursuing the development of a large-scale 2.2GW offshore wind project [111, 118, 122]. Hana Bank is also actively supporting the financial sector, having secured half of the renewable energy financing arrangement market and participating in offshore wind project financing [115]. Furthermore, attempts to utilize railway sound barriers or idle land as renewable energy production sites [101] and Sinan County's rural energy income model, which realizes basic income from renewable energy profits [126, 128, 129], are noteworthy examples of innovative approaches. The European Union demonstrated its acceleration of green transition by sourcing 45.5% of its electricity from renewable energy in the first quarter of 2026 [142].

sections.title: 🌍 Strengthening Climate Change Research and Global Cooperation
sections.content: International research and cooperation are also actively underway to enhance understanding of climate change and explore response measures. China has launched its 16th Arctic expedition focusing on climate change [161, 166], and the second satellite from Victoria University has been launched to advance climate change research [165], strengthening the scientific foundation. From microscopic approaches like the Massachusetts blueberry bush study [158] to macroscopic research findings that air conditioning alone cannot solve climate change [163], the complexity of climate change is being analyzed from various angles. International support programs, such as story grants for Thai companies to report on climate change responses [164], encourage responsible corporate behavior, and discussions on the future costs of climate change [170] and concerns about food loss in Africa [171] emphasize the need for a joint international response.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has demonstrated both the severity of the climate crisis and the coexistence of various stakeholders' efforts to overcome it. We hope that this sense of urgency will lead to stronger and more practical climate actions. This concludes today's climate briefing.
